case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a route from assumptions to target where the compact evidence contains a plausible scalar or local check but omits at least one decisive matrix, dimension, domain, or theorem obligation.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can frame the route boundary, required obligations, and abstention condition without giving an evaluator-only label.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: Do not put expected_output_family or evidence_class labels in prompt text; describe obligations as task context only.

case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a symbolic claim where a backend certificate or concrete witness is decisive only after notation aliases, domain restrictions, or scope boundaries are reconciled.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can provide a notation map and decision criteria; B remains a compact witness/certificate packet that is easy to misread.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: A must not include the decisive witness or certificate; C must not say the evaluator outcome as an authority label.

case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a likelihood, score, optimization, or route-validity claim where several assumptions are route-required and one missing assumption changes whether the conclusion is well posed.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can organize the assumption ledger and non-claims; B supplies compact equations or observations without prioritizing route obligations.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: Scoring labels remain manifest-only; prompt text may ask for assumptions but must not include evaluator-only expected-family fields.

case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a formula-to-code trace with one missing implementation term or ambiguous alias, where prose-only completion is insufficient without a matched/missing-term ledger.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can frame the audit artifact and local/global claim boundary; B lists compact AST or token evidence.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: The prompt may show formula/code summaries but must not assert global code correctness or incorrectness.

case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a local derivation with multiple plausible gaps where the correct output must localize the first failing step and state the missing condition or invalid transformation.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can frame the first-failing-step discipline and downstream handoff; B provides a terse derivation trace.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: C may include the trace and checking protocol, but not an evaluator-only 'first failing step' label.

case_design_requirements.requirement: Include a difficult derivation where the correct output is a diagnostic review packet with assumptions, known checks, gaps, and next artifacts, not proof or final derivation.
case_design_requirements.why_c_should_help: C can specify packet sections and boundary policy; B gives scattered compact evidence that may invite a prose proof overclaim.
case_design_requirements.leakage_guard: Prompt text must not claim packet completeness as proof.

cross_cutting_requirements.requirement: Every case must identify which artifact type is required for a full score: backend certificate, concrete witness, equation-to-code trace, assumption ledger, first-gap localization, or diagnostic packet.
At least three cases must be predeclared as high C-sensitivity because the C prompt should help with routing, assumptions, boundary discipline, or next-artifact actionability.
A prompts must remain insufficient for full-score cases that require decisive executable/source evidence.
B prompts must include compact evidence but omit human-framed packet fields, route-prioritization prose, recommended next action, and narrative answer.
C prompts may include background, decision criteria, assumptions, route gaps, and requested artifact structure, but must not include hidden scoring labels as authority.
No case may require substantial private source excerpts when a synthetic fixture or bounded local summary is enough.
Future collection must use no hidden retries and must preserve malformed outputs rather than replacing them.

non_claims: Difficulty requirements are not scored benchmark results.
Difficulty requirements do not prove v2 will separate B and C.
Difficulty requirements do not authorize response collection.
Difficulty requirements do not establish tool improvement, model reliability, release readiness, public benchmark validity, scientific validation, or product capability.
